reviews.review_msg: Susan is an amazing prof, who lectures with clarity and has very easy-to-follow slides. Always super encouraging of students' interpretations and suggestions. One gets the sense that she fully understands what it is like for a university student to be daunted by having to make insightful literary analysis, but she totally assuages such concerns.
Always willing to meet with students and give detailed feedback on your work. The course material was interesting and the lectures were very detailed and easy to follow.

Susan is a great professor. She is professional and a fair marker; I really respect her. Her lectures are well-organized and participation is encouraged. I learned a lot in her class. If you can take a course with her, I would do it!
Eloquent, insightful, straightforward, and cares for her students.

Susan was a great professor - you can tell that she really cares about her students and the subject matter. Although at times, lectures could be somewhat dull and too straightforward. The texts chosen were very interesting and fun to read. She loves participation though - so get ready to speak up!

Dr. Brook is incredibly sweet and helpful. English lectures are usually boring but she tries her best to keep things mildly entertaining by showing related videos; she also gives solid advice on how to structure writing. The texts she chose for the course were interesting enough. I went to her office hours once and found her advice very helpful.

Eng 115 Susan Brooks is a very good professor. Her lectures are very interesting and engaging. Course material was very good and I really enjoyed the required novels. Two weeks was given to each novel study and it was plenty of time. My TA was good at engaging the students in tutorial but her marking was extremely harsh.
Susan is a really caring professor who definitely supplies her students with the tools for success. Her short story selections were very interesting and good to read.

Susan is honestly one of my favourite profs at SFU, she genuinely cares about her students & is always willing to go the extra mile to help them. She chose four books, and you are given 2 weeks to read each one, and then two short stories. You also have to maintain a reading journal for each book. Despite the amount of work, this is class is great!

Susan is a fabulous professor. She teaches dense material but finds a way to make it relevant and interesting. Expect a ton of reading and a lot of lively group discussion, attendance is mandatory. She is very helpful in office hours and really cares about her students. You will learn a lot and have a good time doing it.

Professor Brook is friendly enough but her instructions in class are very vague and confusing and her responses to in class comments and answers to her questions are  discouraging - makes you feel less inclined to answer because she tends to shut you down or attempt to reword your answer into something you didn't intend.
Pros: Clear about what expectations and assignments. Very helpful if you pay attention. Good lecturer; keeps you interested. Cons: None.

Super charismatic and well spoken, boring powerpoints though. Keep up with readings and writing journals and you'll do fine. The books she chose for my semester (Violence - Fight Club, Persepolis, Extremely Loud and Incredibly Close, Disgrace) were awesome! Within the last decade and all had movies (but try to read the books, they're different)

Susan was a person who reiterated many of the ideas that were blatantly stated in the novel. Her lectures were very abstract and anything said in-class was not allowed to be used for your essays. She limits you on what you can write and the TAs were horrible. Essays were marked extremely hard with no real rubric! DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS! GPA KILLER

She was my TA as well as lecturer, and I liked the tutorial but her grading was very hard. She has high and specific standards, but due to the courses I'm taking this term I'm actually glad she pushed me so much to improve my writing. The course overall was ok but I only liked a couple of the books. It was much harder than i thought it would be.

Her lectures were extremely boring. Most of the time, I felt like she was stretching some of the content she was discussing. (But at least she's passionate about the subject - I personally didn't feel engaged though).

I stopped attending her lectures after the first few classes, her accent was hard to listen and the microphone was terrible. What she explained was a bit confusing too. But overall, the course is easy.

Theories brought up in lecture were interesting but hard to wrap your head around. A lot of "deep" analysis involved. A picky marker -- she finds flaws in essays and magnifies them so that the whole essay has to be changed for the problems to be fixed. Some people do enjoy her way of thinking, but I wouldn't take this class again.

Very helpful and engaging. Encourages questions and promotes discussion in class. Tutorials can be interesting if you choose to participate and keep up with readings! The books we read were really interesting and though-provoking and overall it was a really fun class if you actually keep up with everything and participate. Pretty easy B+ at least.

To be honest, Susan is one of my favourite profs at SFU. She's a great teacher, super into what she's teaching, and makes everything as interesting as possible. the way she talks about each of the novels gets you really engaged in the course. I definitely recommend taking English 101 with her.

Hated the course content. However, Susan seemed passionate about the subject. I did not like how in depth she analyzed the novels that we read. DO NOT take this course if you don't like to read, we read 4 novels. You will succeed if you write "deep" analytical essays.
Love her accent so much. So nice to listen too and brings forth great ideas about the novels

Her lectures are organized and she's easy to understand. Repeats important things to make sure its clear to students which is helpful. However lectures are useless because they want you to develop your own ideas for papers and assignments. Grade depends on the TA you get. Attend tutorial its extremely helpful and interesting if you participate.
Lectures not necessary to attend, seemed very interested in the topics though.. Just attend tutorials and read the stories! Grade all depends on the TA.

Her lectures were interesting, but to be honest they weren't necessary unless you really didn't understand the assigned readings. Tutorials were mandatory and definitely helpful. Overall the class was interesting and pretty easy.

Susan is my favourite teacher in the department, and I was glad to take another class with her. She is super approachable, kind, and willing to give students the push they need to help them succeed. Her lectures are always interesting and the four hours a week just fly by! Take a class with Susan, you won't regret it in the slightest.
I don't know about you guys but this class was just sooooo boring for me. I won't ever take a class with her again.
Susan was a kind and articulate professor. She made class content interesting and spoke with a charismatic tone. Definitely take this class with her!

Susan is amazing, and genuinely wants her students to do well and succeed. She is kind, inspiring, and makes learning relevant and fun. I could go on and on, but won't. Take a class with her; you will not regret it. The two hour seminars fly by!
shes good and nice but its not necessary to go to class. tutorials are very mandatory but its fairly easy. and entertaining. she always shows youtube clips.

This professor is one of the best in the department. Susan is kind, funny, approachable, and generates wonderful discussions that are relatable to the world around us, teaching us to think critically about the world in a fun way. She's so stylish as well! Beautiful inside and out :)

I had low expectations for a fiction course focusing on short stories but she chose ones that were interesting (and somewhat disturbing at times.) Engaging in lectures, she is not only articulate and intelligent but also funny as hell, well worth getting up at 8:30 for.

Long class, but not Susan's fault.  She made the material really interesting and I looked forward to coming to class!  Lively, smart, open to students' viewpoints.  I appreciated the fact she assigned a range of readings outside the textbook.

While I do agree that Susan does not answer emails (and that gets very frusterating) I am confused as to what textbook the previous person was referring to, as we only read books and read them all. Susan is wonderful, and yes, while a bit disorganized, totally worth overlooking that bad part and focusing on the amazing characteristics she has.

TOTALLY DISORGANIZED.Does NOT reply to student emails EVER.Had the class buy a $75 text book&used it 3xs.Made us print approx 2-10 pg.articles a week for the class at our own expense & would not email them until the PM before.She's nice & can  create great discussion/knows the information,but overall is a mess and needs to get her act together.

Definitely my favourite class this semester! She generates great discussions for the class which makes the two hours twice a week just fly by! Would recommend her to anyone! As long as you're a responsible student and actually do the reading you'll do great in this class. Anyone who doesn't like Susan obviously doesn't do the required readings. :)

Really great professor- funny, engaging, and very passionate about her subject. Without a doubt, my favourite class this semester- she really does well at generating great discussions.I can't wait to take her again.

All of the work is graded by your TA. My TA was pretty great and was one of the best for my first semester. English is pretty subjective though, so it can be a hit or miss. Lectures are not uploaded, so be prepared to go to class. I missed quite a few classes since it was 8:30, but I managed a B.

Susan is an excellent professor. She really cares for the students and she puts a lot of time and effort into making the course enjoyable. She generally has 4 or 5 film screenings throughout the course, which makes things a bit more fun. We even watched "The Office" for the entire duration of a 2-hour class.

notes are too brief and so u need to rely on ur ears..and never upload them on the internet so U HAV TO GO TO CLASS. she tries to make the class interesting by showing clips from youtube. i didnt really learn much from her class and often quite repetitive(fiction helps us view the world blahblah) but she's pretty nice =)
Amazing, stimulating, inspiring.
best professor in the english department.
Susan is dynamic, caring, and a wonderful facilitator. She is very good at making challenging material accessible.  She's also brilliant and makes you feel brilliant. Definitely one of my favourites.

Horrible prof. Lectures seem interesting at first, but as the time goes by they become repetative and boring.  Hard marker, doesn't give you enough feedback for your next essay.  Always late for class, and then keeps you in for 5 or 10 minutes after class ends.  I do not recommend her!!!

prof knows her stuff, approachable, definitely see her in office hrs regarding essays, lectures are interesting, really good course books, readings are light, tries to make class enjoyable and succeeds! one of the better english profs at SFU, there are many others to avoid, so dont shy away from this course or prof.
Great prof.  Lively, creative lectures, and also helpful in her office hours.

All I learned in this class is that the world is corrupted and confusing. Very useless class. She marks incredibly HARD. Lectures at first seem very interesting, however, topics repeat, and I feel like I've been sitting in one lecture since all the lectures are so similar, therefore, becomes boring. Looking for an easy course? Don't take this one.

Susan is a wicked prof. her classes are very interesting and engaging and you can tell that she tries hard to make it that way by relating course material to many different aspects of popular culture. Definately consider taking a class with her.

Susan is a really good prof, she cares about her students, and expects you to try your best. Her classes are extremely informative and interesting. You have to work for your mark. Then again, isn't that why we're in school?

She is a decent marker not too hard. She is a good proff you can tell she goes out of her way to make lectures informative and interesting. She is also easy to approach and talk to. Although a warning to those of you who don't usually talk in class she will pick on you.
Tough marker. Lots of writing assignments in her class. But she's an excellent teacher and all her lectures are interesting. Learned a lot from her.
Rough marker, expects the highest quality and will call you on last minute essay writing.  Very approachable and goes out of her way to make the class interesting. Fun person.
